prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Atom‐condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰, and dual descriptor of furan; headline number reported below is the largest f⁺ value.
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ ⇒ B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI (density-fitted) two-electron integrals (PySCF backend)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz”); single-point calculations on the fixed geometry (no further optimisation).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet for N electrons (0, 1); cation state +1/2 (charge +1, doublet); anion state −1/2 (charge −1, doublet).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF 2.x under the hood, via chemkit engine).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for N, N+1, and N−1 states all converged (integrity.status = "ok").  The following engine warnings were issued and are reproduced verbatim:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
No other warnings reported.  The integrity.trustworthy flag returned **true**.

Selected results (atoms numbered as in the input .xyz):
– Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺): atom 5 (C) with f⁺ = 1.9242931.
– Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻): atom 5 (C) with f⁻ = 0.1616371.
Full per-atom f⁺/f⁻/f⁰ and dual descriptor arrays are available in “/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpotq62hxx/live.json”.
